label: agentdb • soft-delete then restore a row
query: Create a table named reminders with a single column text (TEXT). Insert three reminders with the values 'a', 'b', and 'c'. Soft-delete the reminder whose text is 'b'. Then restore it. Finally, confirm that all three reminders are active again and tell me how many active reminders there are.
notes: Soft-delete contract proof: db_delete sets _deleted_at (recoverable) and db_restore clears it. mustCallToolsInOrder pins delete-before-restore. dbState reads the per-eval-agent DB: after the restore all three rows are active (_deleted_at IS NULL), and none are hard-deleted (total still 3).

dbState.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reminders WHERE _deleted_at IS NULL
